items.headline: Health and Safety Element of General Plan under review
items.summary: City Council is considering updates to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision) on May 19. This element typically covers emergency response, hazard mitigation, and public safety policy for the next planning period.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This is a significant leadership decision for Milpitas city operations and governance.

items.headline: Sunnyvale to review draft water shortage plan and master plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These plans shape how Sunnyvale prepares for and responds to water scarcity, a key concern for South Bay residents facing ongoing drought conditions.

items.headline: City to award 2026 community and neighborhood grants
items.summary: Sunnyvale will consider spring cycle applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program on May 18. Local organizations and neighborhood groups can apply for funding to support community events and neighborhood projects.

items.headline: Council to consider residential electrification program principles
items.summary: City Council will review proposed principles for residential electrification programs designed to help comply with Air District regulations. The staff presentation will address the city's electrification status and framework for future programs.

items.headline: Council to update accessory dwelling unit regulations
items.summary: City Council will consider amending zoning code sections governing accessory and junior accessory dwelling units to reflect changes in state law and prior council direction. This affects what types of secondary housing units are permitted on residential properties.

items.headline: Council to update stream corridor protection ordinance
items.summary: City Council will review amendments to stream corridor protection regulations in the municipal code, though the specific changes were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: Auto sales use proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: Planning Commission will consider a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The project involves site improvements like repaving and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior changes.
